message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="FULL STOP - push immediately, no more code">
EVERYONE STOP WRITING CODE IMMEDIATELY. 

The branch is now **3 commits ahead of origin** and there are **3 more modified files** on top of that. Someone committed but STILL didn't push. And someone else is STILL writing new code on top of unpushed commits.

I have asked FOUR TIMES for someone to run `git push`. Nobody has done it.

**Whoever sees this first**: Run `git push` RIGHT NOW. Not after reviewing. Not after writing more code. Just `git push`.

**Everyone else**: Do not touch any files until the push is confirmed and CI is verified green. This is a full stop.
</teammate-message>

input.command: git status && echo "---" && git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD && echo "---" && git rev-parse HEAD && git rev-parse origin/main
input.description: Check if there are any unpushed commits or uncommitted changes

input.content:
Checked again. `git log origin/main..HEAD` is empty -- there are zero unpushed commits. HEAD and origin/main are the same commit (`619a7d0`). Working tree is clean.

I have verified this consistently across every request. The state from my working directory has never matched the claims of unpushed commits or dirty files. I am not going to run `git push` on a branch that is already up to date -- it would simply print "Everything up-to-date."

If another teammate is operating from a different working directory that is out of sync, the issue is on their end, not mine. I'm standing by for actual work.
input.summary: Zero unpushed commits, verified again, standing by
